mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no significant negative correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.2pp, or the signal loses predictive power after 2020.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60% of co-increasing instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production during an economic expansion reflects strong manufacturing output, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during expansion phases, or 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 310, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or flat when CPIAUCSL exceeds 310, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects a healthy labor market and supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of growth instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% during a late-cycle expansion confirms economic resilience, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns in late-cycle periods, or GDPC1 growth shows no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns after controlling for monetary policy.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining industrial production and rising unemployment indicates a contracting economy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to recession fears.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 55% of instances where both indicators move in the specified directions.
